title: Introduction
description: NOC 64301 – Bartenders | GTR Immigration NOC 64301 (Bartenders) are the skilled artisans of Canada's vibrant nightlife and hospitality industry. From craft cocktail bars to high-volume event spaces, bartenders manage everything from inventory and mixology to social safety. As a TEER 4 occupation, bartending is one of the most accessible and high-demand roles for international workers. GTR Immigration helps skilled bartenders leverage their experience to qualify for PR through the Alberta Opportunity Stream and other hospitality-focused provincial pathways.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level4Major Group64 – Sales and service representativesSub-Major Group643 – Occupations in travel and hospitalityMinor Group6430 – Occupations in travel and hospitalityNOC Code64301

duties: Prepare mixed drinks and serve wine and draught or bottled beer.
Take beverage orders from serving staff or directly from patrons.
Maintain an inventory of bar supplies and order additional items as needed.
Clean and sanitize the bar area and wash glassware.
Ensure legal requirements for the responsible service of alcohol are met (e.g., checking IDs).
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Completion of a bartender training course or several years of experience as a server is typically required.
Responsible beverage service certification (e.g., Smart Serve, Serving It Right) is mandatory in all provinces.
job_outlook: The outlook is Very Good. Over 30,000 new job openings are projected due to industry growth and high turnover, making this one of the most stable entry points for new immigrants.
labour_market_demand: The demand for bartenders is currently Excellent across Canada. The labor shortage in the hospitality sector has led to competitive wages and high tips. Demand is particularly critical in tourism hubs like Banff, Whistler, and the downtown cores of major cities.
High Earning Potential: Base wages are often significantly augmented by high tips in the Canadian service culture.
Social Environment: Work in dynamic settings and meet a diverse range of people.
Industry Training: Access to specialized mixology certifications and responsible service training (e.g., Smart Serve, Serving It Right).
pathways: Alberta Opportunity Stream: A very popular pathway for bartenders working in Alberta.
BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled: Specialized stream for hospitality workers.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): Addressing tourism needs in the Atlantic provinces.
